threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think stopping at the corner shop for milk is quick; locals know it takes at least 20 minutes to haggle over the price.
Last night on the 35, a lad tried to sell a deck of cards to a sleeping woman. He nearly went for it when the driver shouted at him to stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy painting a mural on a wall while his dog laughed at pigeons. I thought, yes, this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to see spontaneous rooftop parties—everyone just brings their own drinks and good vibes. You won't find that vibe anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they know the BART delays like the back of their hand—while newcomers just look lost, waiting for a bus that won't show.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 21 doing yoga in the back seat, balancing on one leg. Everyone just pretended it was normal. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell but miss the real charm of Gràcia's hidden plazas, where the locals sip café and gossip under the trees.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them at the bakery, staring at the menu while everyone else orders without a second thought, clutching their Google Translate app.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always tell by how you pronounce 'Mare Street'—the locals say it like 'Mare' not 'Mary.' It's a dead giveaway.
Saw a guy on the 36 coming back from a late shift—he’s fallen asleep in the corner with a takeaway box still in his hand. It was half-open, chips everywhere.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy juggling fireballs outside the U-Bahn station while a couple tried to sell handmade bracelets—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how on U-Bahn platforms, people just chill on the stairs instead of moving to the side? It's like a local culture of standing still.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can spot a tourist a mile away, especially when they’re staring at their phones near a food truck.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of someone in a costume trying to fit through the back door on the 47. The driver was just shaking his head, like this happens daily.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. But the real charm is hidden in the local bars, where the best tapa secrets live.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them staring bewildered at the tram map, holding it upside down while juggling a bag of "patatas bravas".
